name: Lindon
description: The Elven-realm west of the Blue Mountains — Forlindon and Harlindon, Mithlond at the Grey Havens. Ocean-facing and ancient, its weather shaped by the Great Sea and by long Elvish keeping. Milder than mortal coasts, cleaner-feeling, the sea-mists more beautiful than they ought to be.

spring.label: Sea-mist on the shore at dawn, burning off to a high clean sky by noon.
Fair and bright with a salt wind off the Belegaer.
Soft showers passing inland off the sea, each one rainbowed.
High grey cloud, the wind steady, the sea pewter to the horizon.
Mist among the woods of Harlindon, the trees ghosting in and out of sight.
An unseasonable still warmth — the Sea calm, gulls drifting high.
A full day of soft grey rain from the Sea — no hardship, only quiet.
A keen westerly off the water, clouds racing, waves breaking white on the strands.

summer.label: Fair and clean, the Sea a deep blue, the air tasting of salt and thyme.
Warm with a steady sea-breeze, the woods of Lindon at their greenest.
Heavy sea-mist before dawn, clearing to brilliance by mid-morning.
Brief sunlit showers passing through — the Elves walk in them unhurried.
Mild overcast, the Sea silver, no rain — a day of quiet greys.
Thunder walking far out on the Sea, never quite coming to land.
Warm rain, the air rich with the scent of wet pine and sea-wrack.

autumn.label: Cool and bright, the Lindon woods in gold and russet to the water's edge.
Dense fog off the Sea, the shore and the woods one unbroken grey.
Steady cool rain from the west, falling through leaves onto wet roots.
A hard salt wind, the Sea grey and heaving, leaves stripped in hours.
Low grey cloud and a damp chill, the light soft and failing early.
Showers and sunshine in turn — each break lighting the gold of the woods.
An autumn sea-storm — great waves on the cliffs, rain driven almost horizontal.

winter.label: Cold and grey, the Sea lead-coloured, a steady raw wind off the water.
Cold rain from the Sea, unrelenting but never quite bitter.
Cold sea-mist folding over the shore, the havens muffled in grey.
Clear and cold, the Sea sharp blue, the Blue Mountains white in the east.
Sleet on a salt wind — uncomfortable weather even for Elves.
A light fall of snow, uncommon here, dissolving where it meets the salt air.
A great westerly gale, the sea white to the horizon, the Elven-lamps bright on the Havens.
A still bright day — rare in winter Lindon, the Sea almost glassy.
